TABLE OF CONTENTS
Item 1. Business
Item 1A. Risk Factors
Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis
Item 1. Business

Overview

NVIDIA is a computing infrastructure company. We design and build the accelerated computing platforms that power AI factories, data centers, and professional visualization workloads worldwide. Our platforms span silicon, systems, software, and services, and are sold to cloud providers, enterprises, and researchers in every major market. We pioneered accelerated computing to solve problems ordinary computers cannot address, and our architecture is now the standard for training and deploying artificial intelligence at scale. Our two operating segments are Compute & Networking and Graphics, and our supply chain spans fabrication, memory, packaging, and test partners across several continents. We invest heavily in research and development to extend our platform advantage across GPUs, CPUs, DPUs, networking, and systems software, and we return capital to shareholders through repurchases.

Item 1A. Risk Factors

Risks Related to Our Industry and Markets

Our business depends on demand for accelerated computing, which may not continue to grow at historical rates, and long product development cycles expose us to forecasting errors that could harm our results of operations.

Risks Related to Demand, Supply and Manufacturing

We depend on third parties to manufacture, assemble, package, and test our products, and any disruption in their operations could reduce our revenue.

Risks Related to Regulatory, Legal and International Operations

Export controls and trade restrictions may limit our ability to sell into certain markets and could materially affect our financial condition.

Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition

Fiscal year 2025 revenue was a record, driven by data center demand for our accelerated computing platforms. Data Center revenue grew on adoption of our architecture by cloud service providers and consumer internet companies. Gross margin expanded on mix, and operating expenses grew primarily from compensation and infrastructure investments supporting growth. We expect continued sequential growth in demand for our data center products.
